subject: Ecology
title: Coyotes in the City
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Coyotes in the City.

hook.text: Coyotes now live in every major city in North America—including downtown Chicago.

buildup.text: They colonized cities after larger predators like wolves were eliminated from the landscape.

discovery.text: Urban coyotes shift to nocturnal habits, crossing highways at night to avoid people.

twist.text: City coyotes eat everything: rats, rabbits, fallen fruit, pet food left on porches.

climax.text: They regulate rodent and goose populations—providing free pest control most residents never see.

punchline.text: The city's best pest controller wears fur.

quiz.question: Why did coyotes spread into cities?
options: Larger predators were removed from the landscape
